docs.title: Welcome to TheRoundersKnowledge
docs.text: This is the documentation site for TheRoundersKnowledge.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Getting Started - Start here to learn the basics
  • Contribute - How to contribute to this project

docs.title: About This Project
docs.text:
TheRoundersKnowledge is a repository for knowledge, documentation, and resources.

Last updated: May 2026

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thanks for your interest in contributing! Here's how to get started.

docs.title: How to Contribute
docs.text:
1. Create a new branch for your changes: 

    git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name

  2. Add or edit documentation in the docs/ folder using Markdown

  3. Update mkdocs.yml if you're adding a new page: 

    nav:
  - Home: index.md
  - Getting Started: getting-started.md
  - Your New Page: your-page.md

  4. Test locally: 

    mkdocs serve

  5. Commit and push your changes: 

    git add .
git commit -m "Add: description of changes"
git push origin feature/your-feature-name

  6. Create a pull request on GitHub

docs.title: Building for Production
docs.text:
To build the static site for deployment: 

mkdocs build

This generates the site/ folder with all HTML files ready for hosting.

docs.title: Style Guide
docs.text:
• Use clear, concise language
  • Include code examples where helpful
  • Keep pages focused on one topic
  • Use proper Markdown formatting

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Welcome! This guide will help you get up and running.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Before you begin, make sure you have: - Python 3.7+ installed - Git - A text editor

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository: 

    git clone https://github.com/yevhenZ-rounds/TheRoundersKnowledge.git
cd TheRoundersKnowledge

  2. Install MkDocs and dependencies: 

    pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material

  3. Build the documentation locally: 

    mkdocs serve

The site will be available at http://localhost:8000

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Check out the Contribute guide to learn how to add content
  • Explore the documentation structure
  • Start adding your own pages!

docs.title: Assets Directory
docs.text: This directory contains images, downloads, and other media files for the documentation.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
assets/
├── images/          # Image files (PNG, JPG, SVG, GIF)
├── downloads/       # Downloadable files (PDF, ZIP, etc.)
└── README.md        # This file

docs.title: How to Add Files
docs.text:
1. Create folders as needed (images/, downloads/, etc.)
  2. Upload files via GitHub web interface
  3. Reference them in your markdown files using relative paths
  4. Commit and trigger the "Deploy MkDocs" action

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
In your markdown file: 

![My Image](../assets/images/example.png)
[Download File](../assets/downloads/document.pdf)

docs.title: Media & Assets Example
docs.text: This page demonstrates how to work with images and downloadable files in your documentation.

docs.title: Basic Image Embedding
docs.text:
To add an image to your documentation:

![Alt Text](../assets/images/your-image.png)

The ../ means "go up one level" from the examples/ folder to reach assets/.

docs.title: Image with Custom Width
docs.text: <img src="../assets/images/your-image.png" alt="Description" width="400">

docs.title: Centered Image
docs.text:
<div style="text-align: center;">
  <img src="../assets/images/your-image.png" alt="Description" width="600">
</div>

docs.title: Simple Text Link
docs.text: [Download User Guide](../assets/downloads/user-guide.pdf)

docs.title: Button-Style Link
docs.text:
<a href="../assets/downloads/document.pdf" class="md-button md-button--primary">
  📥 Download PDF
</a>

docs.title: File Organization
docs.text:
Your assets should be organized like this:

docs/
├── assets/
│   ├── images/
│   │   ├── screenshot-1.png
│   │   ├── logo.svg
│   │   └── diagram.jpg
│   ├── downloads/
│   │   ├── user-guide.pdf
│   │   └── template.docx
│   └── README.md
├── index.md
├── guides/
└── examples/
    └── media-and-assets.md

docs.title: Step-by-Step: Adding Files to GitHub
docs.text:
1. Navigate to the folder in your GitHub repo where you want to add files
  2. Click "Add file" button (top right)
  3. Choose "Upload files"
  4. Drag and drop your files or click to select them
  5. Write a commit message (e.g., "Add: product screenshot")
  6. Click "Commit changes"
  7. Go to Actions tab and click "Run workflow" on "Deploy MkDocs"
  8. Your changes will be live in a few minutes!

docs.title: Tips for File Management
docs.text: Tip Details Image Formats Use PNG for graphics/screenshots, JPG for photos File Names Use lowercase with hyphens: my-document.pdf Optimize Size Compress images before uploading (keep under 500KB) Alt Text Always provide descriptions for accessibility Organization Group files by type (images/, downloads/, etc.)

docs.title: Images
docs.text:
• ✅ PNG (recommended)
  • ✅ JPG/JPEG
  • ✅ SVG
  • ✅ GIF
  • ✅ WebP

docs.title: Documents
docs.text:
• ✅ PDF
  • ✅ TXT
  • ✅ Markdown

docs.title: Archives
docs.text:
• ✅ ZIP
  • ✅ TAR
  • ✅ RAR

docs.title: Example File Structure
docs.text:
To try this yourself:

  1. Create a docs/assets/images/ folder
  2. Upload an image file there
  3. Reference it in this page: 
    ![My Image](../assets/images/my-image.png)
  4. Commit and deploy!

Next: Check the Markdown Syntax Guide to learn more formatting options!

docs.title: Adding Files and Media
docs.text: Learn how to add images, PDFs, and other files to your documentation.

docs.title: Directory Structure for Assets
docs.text:
Create an assets folder in the docs/ directory:

docs/
├── assets/
│   ├── images/
│   │   └── screenshot.png
│   ├── downloads/
│   │   └── document.pdf
│   └── files/
│       └── example.zip
├── index.md
└── guides/
    └── adding-files.md

docs.title: Inline Image
docs.text: ![Alt text](../assets/images/screenshot.png)

docs.title: Image with Link
docs.text: [![Alt text](../assets/images/screenshot.png)](https://example.com)

docs.title: Centered Image (HTML)
docs.text:
<div style="text-align: center;">
  <img src="../assets/images/screenshot.png" alt="Description" width="600">
</div>

docs.title: PDF Download
docs.text: [Download PDF](../assets/downloads/document.pdf)

docs.title: ZIP File Download
docs.text: [Download Files](../assets/files/example.zip)

docs.title: Button-style Download Link
docs.text:
<a href="../assets/downloads/document.pdf" class="md-button md-button--primary">
  Download PDF
</a>

docs.title: File Organization Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Keep assets organized - Use subdirectories by type (images, downloads, files)
  2. Use descriptive names - user-guide.pdf instead of doc1.pdf
  3. Optimize images - Compress before uploading to keep repo size small
  4. Relative paths - Use ../ to reference parent directories

docs.title: Images
docs.text:
• PNG (recommended for diagrams)
  • JPG (good for photos)
  • SVG (scalable vector graphics)
  • GIF (animated)
  • WebP (modern format)

docs.title: Documents
docs.text:
• PDF
  • Markdown files
  • Text files

docs.title: Archives
docs.text:
• ZIP
  • TAR.GZ
  • 7Z

docs.title: Adding Files via GitHub
docs.text:
1. Go to your repo on GitHub
  2. Navigate to the folder where you want to add files
  3. Click Add file → Upload files
  4. Drag and drop your files or click to select
  5. Add a commit message
  6. Commit to main branch
  7. Run the Deploy MkDocs action to rebuild your site

docs.title: Example: Adding an Image
docs.text:
1. Create folder: docs/assets/images/
  2. Upload your image via GitHub
  3. In your markdown file, add: 
    ![My Screenshot](../assets/images/screenshot.png)
  4. That's it!

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Keep image filenames lowercase with hyphens: my-screenshot.png
  • Use Alt text for accessibility
  • For large files, consider using external storage and linking to it

docs.title: Markdown Syntax Guide
docs.text: Learn how to format your documentation with Markdown.

docs.title: Headings
docs.text:
# Heading 1
## Heading 2
### Heading 3
#### Heading 4
##### Heading 5
###### Heading 6

docs.title: Text Formatting
docs.text:
Bold text - wrap with **text**

Italic text - wrap with *text*

Bold and italic - wrap with ***text***

~~Strikethrough~~ - wrap with ~~text~~

docs.title: Unordered Lists
docs.text:
• Item 1
  • Item 2
  • Nested item 2.1
  • Nested item 2.2
  • Item 3

docs.title: Ordered Lists
docs.text:
1. First item
  2. Second item
  3. Nested item 2.1
  4. Nested item 2.2
  5. Third item

docs.title: Inline Code
docs.text: Use backticks for inline code like variable names or functions.

docs.title: Code Blocks
docs.text:
def hello_world():
    print("Hello, World!")
    return True

function add(a, b) {
    return a + b;
}

git commit -m "Your message"
git push origin main

docs.title: Tables
docs.text: Feature Supported Notes Formatting ✅ Bold, italic, code Links ✅ Internal and external Images ✅ PNG, JPG, SVG, GIF Code blocks ✅ Syntax highlighting

docs.title: Checklists
docs.text:
• [x] Completed task
  • [ ] Incomplete task
  • [x] Another completed task

docs.title: Tips and Notes
docs.text:
!!! note This is a note. Use it for additional information.

!!! warning This is a warning. Use it for important cautions.

!!! success This is a success message.

!!! danger This is a danger message.
